summary: DHH celebrates a backhanded milestone: Ruby on Rails appearing as a bullet point on a corporate job listing. The job posting absurdly lumps Rails alongside J2EE and Websphere, demanding 5 years experience with 'the latest and greatest tools' and the ability to construct 'Enterprise wide Java solutions from whitepapers.' DHH finds it hilariously ironic that Rails has been adopted by the very corporate recruiting culture it stands against. He treats this as proof that Rails has broken through the mainstream awareness barrier.
key_points: Rails appearing on corporate job listings signals mainstream technology adoption, even if the context is absurd
The job posting perfectly captures clueless enterprise recruiting: demanding experience with contradictory technologies and buzzword soup
The phrase 'constructing Enterprise wide Java solutions from whitepapers' epitomizes the bloated enterprise culture Rails was built to reject
True technology awareness is reached when even people who don't understand a tool start requiring it
money_quotes: On the ladder of corporate acceptance, few achievements rank higher than to bestowed the honor of becoming a bullet point on the recruiting check list.
Should have experience constructing Enterprise wide Java solutions from whitepapers.
Rails has truly broken through the awareness barrier when its included as part of a job application that's the antithesis of everything Rails is about.
Clueless recruiters of the world, I salute you!
Should have ideally 5 years experience with all of the latest and greatest tools out there. The more the better.
key_insight: A technology has truly gone mainstream when it gets mindlessly listed on corporate job postings by recruiters who have no idea what it actually represents.
